This script calculates descriptive statistics (mean ± SEM, median, SD, n) for changes in extracellular potassium concentration and changes in the potassium Nernst potential recorded by a potassium-sensitive electrode during SLA experiments. Baseline potassium concentration in the solution was 3 mM.

# load data
df <- read.csv("OHSC_Potassium_Increase_SLA.csv")

# descriptive statistic
get_stats <- function(data) {
  data <- na.omit(data)
  mean_val <- mean(data)
  sem_val  <- sd(data) / sqrt(length(data))
  cat(sprintf("Mean ± SEM: %.2f ± %.2f\n", mean_val, sem_val))
  cat(sprintf("Median: %.2f\n", median(data)))
  cat(sprintf("SD: %.2f\n", sd(data)))
  cat(sprintf("n = %d\n", length(data)))
}

# stats for delta_Potential
cat("\nDelta Potential\n")
get_stats(df$delta_Potential)

# stats Delta K+
cat("\nDelta K+ (+ 3 mM baseline corrected)\n")
get_stats(df$Delta_K. + 3)
